# Test apx catalog generate command
#
# This scenario verifies that `apx catalog generate` scans git tags
# matching <format>/<domain>/<name>/<line>/v<semver> and produces
# a catalog.yaml with correct identity, version, and lifecycle fields.

# Setup: Create a test canonical repo with tags
mkdir canonical
cd canonical
exec git init
exec git config user.email 'test@test.com'
exec git config user.name 'Test'
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=apis --skip-git --non-interactive

# Create an initial commit so tags work
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init'

# Create release tags simulating published APIs
exec git tag proto/payments/ledger/v1/v1.0.0
exec git tag proto/payments/ledger/v1/v1.1.0
exec git tag proto/payments/ledger/v1/v1.2.3
exec git tag proto/payments/wallet/v1/v1.0.0-beta.1
exec git tag proto/payments/wallet/v1/v1.0.0-beta.2
exec git tag openapi/iam/roles/v2/v2.0.0-alpha.1
exec git tag openapi/iam/roles/v2/v2.0.0-alpha.3

# Run catalog generate
exec apx catalog generate --org=testorg --repo=apis
stdout 'Catalog generated'
stdout '3 APIs discovered'

# Verify catalog file was written
exists catalog/catalog.yaml

# Verify stable API (ledger) has correct latest version and lifecycle
exec grep 'id: proto/payments/ledger/v1' catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'latest_stable: v1.2.3' catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'lifecycle: stable' catalog/catalog.yaml

# Verify beta API (wallet) has correct lifecycle
exec grep 'id: proto/payments/wallet/v1' catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'latest_prerelease: v1.0.0-beta.2' catalog/catalog.yaml

# Verify alpha API (roles) lifecycle is experimental
exec grep 'id: openapi/iam/roles/v2' catalog/catalog.yaml
exec grep 'latest_prerelease: v2.0.0-alpha.3' catalog/catalog.yaml

# Generate with custom output path
exec apx catalog generate --org=testorg --repo=apis -o custom/output.yaml
stdout 'Catalog generated'
exists custom/output.yaml

# Generate with JSON output
exec apx catalog generate --org=testorg --repo=apis --json
stdout '"Version": 1'
stdout '"Org": "testorg"'
stdout '"Modules"'
